delivery-adapter-generic.label: Generic delivery adapter registry coverage
delivery-adapter-generic.expected_behavior: The generic collector remains selectable through the shared adapter registry and dispatcher allowlist.
manual_verification: Open Settings > Delivery and confirm Generic Collector appears in the adapter selector.

delivery-adapter-sgtm.label: sGTM adapter registry coverage
delivery-adapter-sgtm.expected_behavior: The sGTM adapter remains selectable through the shared adapter registry and dispatcher allowlist.
manual_verification: Open Settings > Delivery and confirm sGTM (Server GTM) appears in the adapter selector.

delivery-adapter-meta.label: Meta adapter registry coverage
delivery-adapter-meta.expected_behavior: Meta CAPI remains selectable through the shared adapter registry and dispatcher allowlist.
manual_verification: Open Settings > Delivery and confirm Meta CAPI appears in the adapter selector.

delivery-adapter-google.label: Google adapter registry coverage
delivery-adapter-google.expected_behavior: Google Ads / GA4 remains selectable through the shared adapter registry and dispatcher allowlist.
manual_verification: Open Settings > Delivery and confirm Google Ads / GA4 appears in the adapter selector.

delivery-adapter-linkedin.label: LinkedIn adapter registry coverage
delivery-adapter-linkedin.expected_behavior: LinkedIn CAPI remains selectable through the shared adapter registry and dispatcher allowlist.
manual_verification: Open Settings > Delivery and confirm LinkedIn CAPI appears in the adapter selector.

delivery-adapter-pinterest.label: Pinterest adapter registry coverage
delivery-adapter-pinterest.expected_behavior: Pinterest Conversions API is selectable through the shared adapter registry and dispatcher allowlist.
manual_verification: Open Settings > Delivery and confirm Pinterest Conversions API appears in the adapter selector.

delivery-adapter-tiktok.label: TikTok adapter registry coverage
delivery-adapter-tiktok.expected_behavior: TikTok Events API is selectable through the shared adapter registry and dispatcher allowlist.
manual_verification: Open Settings > Delivery and confirm TikTok Events API appears in the adapter selector.

destination-meta-toggle.label: Meta destination toggle wiring
destination-meta-toggle.expected_behavior: Meta appears in the Events tab through the shared destination registry.
manual_verification: Enable Meta in Events and save. Reload the screen and confirm the toggle persists.

destination-google-toggle.label: Google destination toggle wiring
destination-google-toggle.expected_behavior: Google appears in the Events tab through the shared destination registry.
manual_verification: Enable Google in Events and save. Reload the screen and confirm the toggle persists.

destination-linkedin-toggle.label: LinkedIn destination toggle wiring
destination-linkedin-toggle.expected_behavior: LinkedIn appears in the Events tab through the shared destination registry.
manual_verification: Enable LinkedIn in Events and save. Reload the screen and confirm the toggle persists.

destination-reddit-toggle.label: Reddit destination toggle wiring
destination-reddit-toggle.expected_behavior: Reddit remains available as a relay-only destination toggle.
manual_verification: Enable Reddit in Events and save. Reload the screen and confirm the toggle persists.

destination-pinterest-toggle.label: Pinterest destination toggle wiring
destination-pinterest-toggle.expected_behavior: Pinterest appears in the Events tab through the shared destination registry.
manual_verification: Enable Pinterest in Events and save. Reload the screen and confirm the toggle persists.

destination-tiktok-toggle.label: TikTok destination toggle wiring
destination-tiktok-toggle.expected_behavior: TikTok appears in the Events tab through the shared destination registry.
manual_verification: Enable TikTok in Events and save. Reload the screen and confirm the toggle persists.

admin-save-capture.label: Grouped admin save/load mapping for Capture
admin-save-capture.expected_behavior: Capture settings map cleanly between the unified admin app and clicutcl_attribution_settings.
manual_verification: Toggle attribution capture or retention values, save, reload, and confirm the values persist.

POST wp-admin/admin-ajax.php with action=clicutcl_save_admin_settings, nonce=<clicutcl_admin_settings nonce>, and a settings JSON payload that changes capture.enabled. Confirm response is {"success":true,"data":{"message":"Settings saved.","settings":{...}}} and the capture group reflects the change.
POST the same endpoint with an invalid or missing nonce. Confirm WordPress returns a -1 or {"success":false} failure — not a 200 with saved data.

After a successful save, POST action=clicutcl_get_admin_settings with the same nonce. Confirm the returned settings.capture object matches what was just saved (round-trip correctness). Confirm sanitize_settings() output is present — no raw unsanitized keys.

admin-save-forms.label: Grouped admin save/load mapping for Forms
admin-save-forms.expected_behavior: Forms settings map cleanly between the unified admin app and the stored option groups.
manual_verification: Toggle client fallback or provider settings, save, reload, and confirm the values persist.

POST wp-admin/admin-ajax.php with action=clicutcl_save_admin_settings, nonce=<clicutcl_admin_settings nonce>, and a settings JSON payload that changes forms.client_fallback. Confirm response is {"success":true,"data":{"message":"Settings saved.","settings":{...}}} and the forms group reflects the change.

After a successful save, POST action=clicutcl_get_admin_settings with the same nonce. Confirm the returned settings.forms object matches what was just saved. Confirm whatsapp and external_webhooks keys are present and sanitized.

admin-get-admin-settings.label: Admin settings GET returns full schema
admin-get-admin-settings.expected_behavior: The clicutcl_get_admin_settings AJAX endpoint returns a success envelope whose settings object contains all expected top-level keys.
prerequisites: Logged in as a manage_options user.
A valid clicutcl_admin_settings nonce is available (localized in the settings app script data).
manual_verification: POST wp-admin/admin-ajax.php with action=clicutcl_get_admin_settings and nonce=<clicutcl_admin_settings nonce>. Confirm response shape is {"success":true,"data":{"settings":{...}}} and the settings object contains keys: capture, forms, events, delivery, status, setup_checklist.
Repeat the request with a missing or invalid nonce. Confirm the response is a WordPress nonce failure (HTTP 400 or {"success":false}) — not a 200 with settings data.
Repeat the request while logged out (no auth cookie). Confirm the response is a WordPress capability failure — not a 200 with settings data.

signed-intake-gate.label: Signed intake auth acceptance and rejection gates
signed-intake-gate.expected_behavior: Browser event intake remains protected by signed tokens, host checks, and nonce replay limits.
manual_verification: Verify that valid signed tokens are accepted and invalid, expired, or replayed tokens are rejected with 401 or 429 responses.

woo-view-item.label: WooCommerce product view browser event
woo-view-item.expected_behavior: Woo product pages emit view_item through the shared browser event layer.
manual_verification: Open a product page and confirm a view_item event appears in dataLayer or debug output.

woo-add-to-cart.label: WooCommerce add_to_cart browser event
woo-add-to-cart.expected_behavior: Woo add-to-cart interactions emit add_to_cart through the shared browser event layer.
manual_verification: Add a product to cart and confirm an add_to_cart event appears in dataLayer or debug output.

woo-remove-from-cart.label: WooCommerce remove_from_cart browser event
woo-remove-from-cart.expected_behavior: Classic and block cart removals emit remove_from_cart through the shared browser event layer.
manual_verification: Remove an item from the classic cart or block cart and confirm a remove_from_cart event appears in dataLayer or debug output.

woo-begin-checkout.label: WooCommerce begin_checkout browser event
woo-begin-checkout.expected_behavior: Woo checkout pages emit begin_checkout through the shared browser event layer.
manual_verification: Open the checkout page and confirm a begin_checkout event appears in dataLayer or debug output.

woo-view-item-list.label: WooCommerce list-view browser event
woo-view-item-list.expected_behavior: Woo product lists emit view_item_list with list-name and list-index context through the shared browser event layer.
manual_verification: Open a shop, category, related-products, or cross-sell surface and confirm a view_item_list event appears with item_list_name and item_list_index fields.

woo-server-view-item.label: WooCommerce server-side view_item funnel event
woo-server-view-item.expected_behavior: Product pages emit one server-side view_item event with a deterministic session-scoped event ID, and the browser layer suppresses its duplicate REST transport.
prerequisites: WooCommerce server-side funnel events are enabled.
manual_verification: Open a product page and confirm a view_item event reaches the server-side queue or adapter logs with a view_item_<product>_<session> event ID.
With storefront events also enabled, confirm the dataLayer view_item push carries the same event_id and no duplicate REST intake request is sent.

woo-server-add-to-cart.label: WooCommerce server-side add_to_cart funnel event
woo-server-add-to-cart.expected_behavior: Cart additions emit one server-side add_to_cart event with a minute-bucketed event ID that collapses redirect and AJAX double-fires while allowing legitimate repeat adds.
prerequisites: WooCommerce server-side funnel events are enabled.
manual_verification: Add a product to the cart and confirm one add_to_cart event reaches the server-side queue or adapter logs.
Add the same product again within the same minute and confirm dedup collapses the second event; repeat after a minute and confirm a new event is accepted.

woo-server-begin-checkout.label: WooCommerce server-side begin_checkout funnel event
woo-server-begin-checkout.expected_behavior: Checkout pages emit one server-side begin_checkout event keyed by session and cart hash so reloads deduplicate while cart changes produce a fresh event.
prerequisites: WooCommerce server-side funnel events are enabled.
manual_verification: Open the checkout page with items in the cart and confirm one begin_checkout event reaches the server-side queue or adapter logs.
Reload checkout and confirm dedup collapses the repeat; change the cart contents and confirm a new begin_checkout event is accepted.

woo-order-paid.label: WooCommerce order_paid milestone
woo-order-paid.expected_behavior: Paid orders emit one server-side order_paid milestone with deterministic event IDs and order markers.
manual_verification: Complete payment for a test order and confirm order_paid appears once in Diagnostics > Woo Order Trace Lookup.

woo-order-refunded.label: WooCommerce order_refunded milestone
woo-order-refunded.expected_behavior: Refunded orders emit one server-side order_refunded milestone with deterministic event IDs and order markers.
manual_verification: Refund a test order and confirm order_refunded appears once in Diagnostics > Woo Order Trace Lookup.

woo-order-cancelled.label: WooCommerce order_cancelled milestone
woo-order-cancelled.expected_behavior: Cancelled orders emit one server-side order_cancelled milestone with deterministic event IDs and order markers.
manual_verification: Cancel a test order and confirm order_cancelled appears once in Diagnostics > Woo Order Trace Lookup.

diagnostics-conflict-scan.label: Diagnostics conflict scan
diagnostics-conflict-scan.expected_behavior: Diagnostics exposes a deterministic conflict scan for caching, destination mismatches, and Woo gaps.
manual_verification: Run the conflict scan and confirm findings render inline when known mismatches exist.

diagnostics-woo-lookup.label: Diagnostics Woo order trace lookup
diagnostics-woo-lookup.expected_behavior: Diagnostics can load stored Woo purchase and milestone traces and enrich them with queue state.
manual_verification: Lookup a valid Woo order ID and confirm purchase or milestone trace snapshots render with queue details.

settings-export-import.label: Settings backup export and restore
settings-export-import.expected_behavior: Diagnostics can export and restore the five main ClickTrail option stores through privileged admin actions.
manual_verification: Export a backup, inspect the JSON metadata, then import it and confirm settings are restored through the admin sanitizers.

queue-retry-semantics.label: Queue retry and backoff semantics
queue-retry-semantics.expected_behavior: Failed dispatches continue to enqueue, back off, and surface queue stats through the shared queue layer.
prerequisites: A failing test endpoint is available.
manual_verification: Force a failing dispatch and confirm the queue stores attempts, next_attempt_at, and last_error values that advance with retries.

gf-channel-field.label: Gravity Forms channel field classification and persistence
gf-channel-field.expected_behavior: GF entries receive ct_ft_channel with a human-readable label derived from click IDs, UTM parameters, or referrer context; server-side fallback computes the label when JS attribution is unavailable.
manual_verification: Submit a GF form after visiting with ?gclid=test. Open the entry detail and confirm ct_ft_channel = 'Google Ads'.
Submit a GF form with no click ID or UTM. Confirm ct_ft_channel is 'Direct' or 'Unknown' — never empty.

gf-expanded-click-ids.label: Expanded click ID capture (Reddit, Pinterest, Snapchat, Mailchimp, DV360)
gf-expanded-click-ids.expected_behavior: The six new click IDs (rdt_cid, pin_cid, snap_cid, mc_cid, mc_eid, dclid) are captured in the attribution cookie and persisted as ct_* GF entry meta alongside existing IDs.
manual_verification: Visit with ?rdt_cid=test and submit a GF form. Confirm ct_rdt_cid appears in the entry detail.
Visit with ?mc_cid=test and submit. Confirm ct_mc_cid is present and ct_ft_channel = 'Mailchimp'.

gf-merge-tags.label: Gravity Forms merge tag resolution from entry meta
gf-merge-tags.expected_behavior: All nine {clicutcl_*} merge tags resolve their stored entry meta values in GF notifications and confirmations, with empty-string fallback and three developer filter hooks.
prerequisites: A GF notification or confirmation contains at least one {clicutcl_*} merge tag.
manual_verification: Add {clicutcl_channel} to a GF notification. Submit the form after visiting with ?gclid=test. Confirm the notification body contains 'Google Ads'.
Confirm {clicutcl_click_id} returns the first non-empty click ID meta value.

gf-per-form-toggle.label: Per-form attribution tracking toggle with global default fallback
gf-per-form-toggle.expected_behavior: Attribution tracking can be disabled per form via a ClickTrail section in GF form settings; the toggle defaults to the global gf_tracking_default_enabled option and can be overridden by the clicutcl_gf_tracking_enabled filter.
manual_verification: Open a GF form in the admin, go to Form Settings, and confirm a ClickTrail section with an enable/disable toggle is present.
Disable tracking for a form, submit it, and confirm no ct_* entry meta is written.

gf-entry-edit-safety.label: Attribution meta preserved after manual GF entry edit
gf-entry-edit-safety.expected_behavior: Manually editing a GF entry does not clear or overwrite ct_* attribution meta; tracking fields are excluded from the edit screen and any values cleared during save are restored.
prerequisites: An entry exists with ct_ft_source or ct_gclid meta set.
manual_verification: Open a GF entry with attribution data and click Edit. Save without touching any field. Confirm ct_* meta values are unchanged.
Confirm that ct_* fields are absent from the editable field list on the entry edit screen.

gf-admin-qa-mode.label: Admin QA mode writes to sessionStorage only — no persistent cookie
gf-admin-qa-mode.expected_behavior: When a manage_options user is logged in, attribution data is written to sessionStorage instead of the persistent cookie and localStorage, preventing admin browsing from polluting real attribution data.
prerequisites: Log in as a WordPress administrator.
manual_verification: Log in as admin, visit the site, open DevTools Application tab. Confirm no ct_attribution cookie is set; confirm sessionStorage contains the attribution object.
Log out, visit the same page. Confirm the ct_attribution cookie is now written normally.

gf-script-minification-protection.label: ClickTrail script tags carry minification-exclusion attributes
gf-script-minification-protection.expected_behavior: The clicutcl-attribution-js, clicutcl-consent-bridge-js, and clicutcl-consent-js script tags are emitted with data-no-optimize, data-cfasync, data-wprocket-exclude, and data-litespeed-no-optimize attributes.
manual_verification: View the rendered page HTML source. Confirm each ClickTrail script tag includes data-no-optimize="1" and data-cfasync="false".

gf-session-storage-fallback.label: sessionStorage fallback when cookies are blocked
gf-session-storage-fallback.expected_behavior: When the attribution cookie cannot be written (cookies blocked by browser policy), attribution data falls back to sessionStorage using the same JSON envelope, remaining readable for the lifetime of the tab.
prerequisites: Cookies are blocked for the test domain (disable via DevTools or browser settings).
manual_verification: Block cookies for the site in DevTools. Visit a URL with ?utm_source=test. Confirm sessionStorage contains the attribution object after page load.
Confirm the attribution data is readable by GF field pre-population on the same page load.
